core_mechanism: Reformulation of a linear differential eigenvalue problem into an equivalent Green's-function integral-equation system, in which the unknown field is represented by integral operators acting only over the source region and its boundary, so that a whole-space PDE is reduced to a bounded-domain integral equation whose critical eigenvalue marks the onset of self-sustained growth
brief_reason: The abstract states that the governing induction (differential) equation is transformed into an equivalent integral equation system that restricts the computational domain to the conducting region and its boundary, and is used to locate the self-excitation (critical-eigenvalue) threshold separating decaying from growing solutions.
